DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS RICHARD TEED, TEED HAZE DEVELOPMENT, LLC'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND SANCTIONS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. DEFENDANT IS A PARTY TO THE LITIGATION AND HAS FILED A CROSS-COMPLAINT, AND THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE REQUIRES THAT HE SIT FOR HIS DEPOSITION IF PROPERLY NOTICED BY THE OPPOSING PARTY. CCP 2025.010. DEFENDANT HAS THE BURDEN OF SHOWING GOOD CAUSE WHY HIS DEPOSITION SHOULD BE POSTPONED. THERE APPEAR TO BE TWO PRIMARY REASONS DEFENDANT HAS TO DELAY THE DEPOSITION: (1) AWAITING AN INSURANCE COVERAGE DETERMINATION; AND (2) AWAITING THE STATUS OF CROSS-DEFENDANT DONALD MOORE. DEFENDANT'S INSURANCE COMPANY HAS VERBALLY AGREED TO PROVIDE COVERAGE (REPLY, 3:21-22), SO THERE DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE ANY FURTHER REASON TO DELAY THE DEPOSITION ON THOSE GROUNDS. CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS FILED A DEMURRER TO THE FAC, WHICH IF SUSTAINED WILL MOOT DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENTS, AND IF IT IS NOT SUSTAINED, CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS THE RIGHT TO DEPOSE DEFENDANT TO PREPARE HIS DEFENSE TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ON THE ISSUES SPECIFIC TO HIM. THERE DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE ANY FURTHER REASON TO DELAY THE DEPOSITION ON THIS BASIS EITHER. ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY ORDERED DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION FOR JULY 17, 2014. DEFENDANT NOW INDICATES THAT HE IS UNAVAILABLE FOR THIS DATE AND COUNSEL WAS NOT ABLE TO CONFER WITH DEFENDANT PRIOR TO THE COURT ORDERING THE DEPOSITION DATE. THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT IS HEREBY CONTINUED TO A MUTUALLY AGREEABLE TIME, BUT IN NO EVENT SHALL IT BEGIN LATER THAN JULY 31, 2014. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS DENIED, THERE DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE AN ABUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS, SO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: CONOR MOORE, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied in its entirety. Sotheby's did not satisfy its initial burden of submitting sufficient facts showing that it is entitled to judgment on any of the three claims for which it seeks summary adjudication and, even if it did, there are numerous disputed material facts precluding summary adjudication as to each of the three claims. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HOM-PISANO ENGINEERING, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied because it is procedurally defective under CCP 437c(f)(1). First, the motion is improper because a claim for damages under CCP 437c(f)(1) refers to a claim for punitive damages. See Catalano v. Sup. Crt., (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 91, 96-97; DeCastro West Chodorow & Burns, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 410, 419. Second, any ruling regarding the limitation of liability clause does not completely dispose of a damages claim. D's Notice of Motion cites CAZA Drilling (CA) Inc. v. TEG Oil & Gas USA, Inc. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 453, 471-472 and Burnett v. Chimney Sweep (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 1057, 1065. Those cases discuss when an exculpatory clause can be enforceable. Those cases do not discuss the procedural summary adjudication issue. Defendant also cites Markborough California, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1991) 227 Cal.App.3d 705, 708. In that case, the lower court granted summary adjudication on the limitation of liability issue. The Markborough court does not discuss the summary adjudication procedural issue and it appears that the decision predates amendments made to CCP 437c(f)(1). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard McDonald, Esq of Hopkins & Carley (408-286-9800) for Justin Moore Plaintiff and opposing party through Court Call.
Brian Soriano, Esq (415-615-0881) for Richard Burdern Teed Defendant and moving party

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs RICHARD TEED, TEED HAZE DEVELOPMENT, LLC's Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions. Plaintiff's requests for judicial notice are granted. Defendant is a party to the litigation and has filed a cross-complaint, and the Code of Civil Procedure requires that he sit for his deposition if properly noticed by the opposing party. CCP 2025.010. Defendant has the burden of showing good cause why his deposition should be postponed. There appear to be two primary reasons Defendant has to delay the deposition: (1) awaiting an insurance coverage determination; and (2) awaiting the status of Cross-Defendant Donald Moore. Defendant's insurance company has verbally agreed to provide coverage (Reply, 3:21-22), so there does not appear to be any further reason to delay the deposition on those grounds. Cross-Defendant has filed a demurrer to the FAC, which if sustained will moot Defendant's arguments, and if it is not sustained, Cross-Defendant has the right to depose Defendant to prepare his defense to the cross-complaint on the issues specific to him. There does not appear to be any further reason to delay the deposition on this basis either. Accordingly, Defendant's motion for a protective order is denied. Defendant's request for sanctions against Defendant is denied. The Court has previously ordered Defendant's deposition for July 17, 2014. Defendant now indicates that he is unavailable for this date and counsel was not able to confer with Defendant prior to the Court ordering the deposition date. The deposition of Defendant is hereby continued to a mutually agreeable time, but in no event shall it begin later than July 31, 2014. Although Defendant's motion is denied, there does not appear to be an abuse of the discovery process, so Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Order is signed in Open Court. Judge Pro Tem: Conor Moore, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS DEFENDANT DONALD MOORE's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) "[U]nless the prospective indemnitor and indemnitee are jointly and severally liable to the plaintiff there is no basis for indemnity." (Yamaha Motor Corp. v. Paseman (1990) 219 Cal.App. 958, 964.) In other words, "indemnity does not invariably follow fault; it is premised on a joint legal obligation to another for damages." (Western Steamship Lines, Inc. v. San Pedro Peninsula Hospital (1994) 8 Cal.4th 100, 114.) Because the Cross-Complaint does not plead facts establishing that Cross-Defendant could be held liable in tort to Plaintiff in the underlying action, the Cross-Complaint does not state a cause of action for equitable indemnity and contribution. The Court signed the proposed order that was submitted by email. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
